description: Confounder: The model assumes ExpertChoiceRouter always assigns exactly batch_size * capacit

_meta.explanation: The rule 'ExpertChoiceRouter's num_tokens_per_expert is always uniformly set to batch_size * capacity, regardless of actual token routing distribution or overlaps.' does NOT hold here. The model assumes ExpertChoiceRouter always assigns exactly batch_size * capacity tokens per expert by preallocating slots uniformly. However, in sparse routing scenarios (e.g., top-1 gating with deterministic logits), actual token assignments depend on gate decisions, and unused expert slots remain empty—so num_tokens_per_expert reflects real routed counts, not preallocated capacity.
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'ExpertChoiceRouter's num_tokens_per_expert is always uniformly set to batch_size * capacity, regardless of actual token routing distribution or overlaps.' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
